arXiv_CondMat.title: Transition Metal Dichalcogenide Excitons in Periodic Electrostatic Potentials: Center-of-Mass Models
arXiv_CondMat.summary: arXiv:2605.03162v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Two-dimensional (2D) van-der-Waals materials are a promising platform for exciton state engi- neering. In this paper, we study the properties of excitons in 2D group VI transition-metal dichalco- genide (TMD) semiconductors that are modified by a periodic electrostatic potential through the quadratic Stark effect. Using a model that retains only center-of-mass and valley degrees-of-freedom, we find that electrostatic potentials can drive optical valley splitting up to 10meVs and induce valley selective exciton dispersion. We explain why both properties are sensitive to the rotational symmetry of the electrostatic trapping potential using a combination of numerical results and an- alytical approximations. An important consequence of valley-splitting is that the lowest exciton band is non-degenerate and has a linear dispersion around gamma that is expected to suppress thermal excitations, allowing true Bose condensation and superfluidity of excitons in two space dimensions.
